---
title: "modelli_riccardo"
author: "Riccardo"
date: "2026-05-09"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, fig.align = 'center')
```


```{r}
pacman::p_load(foreign, tidyverse, forcats, survey, srvyr, lme4, lmerTest, knitr, 
               kableExtra, gt, gtsummary, scales, patchwork, ggeffects, palettes, choroplethr, choroplethrMaps)

df <- read.csv("brfss24_sub.csv")
```


```{r}
df %>% 
  group_by(X_STATE) %>% 
  summarise(n = n(),
            prop = round((n/nrow(df))*100,2)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(n))
```


```{r}

# Calcola la proporzione di mammografia per stato
state_mam <- df %>%
  filter(X_SEX == "Female", !is.na(HADMAM), !is.na(X_STATE)) %>%
  group_by(X_STATE) %>%
  summarise(
    n     = n(),
    value = mean(HADMAM == 1, na.rm = TRUE)  # proporzione di Yes
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    region = tolower(X_STATE) # la funzione vuole i nomi in minuscolo
  ) %>%
  select(region, value)

nordest <- c("connecticut", "massachusetts",
             "rhode island", "vermont", "new hampshire",
             "maine")

# La mappa
state_choropleth(
  state_mam,
  title  = "Mammography participation by State",
  legend = "Proportion",
  zoom   = nordest
  )


```





## References

- [https://ikwak2.github.io/tutorials/BRFSS/BRFSS.html](https://ikwak2.github.io/tutorials/BRFSS/BRFSS.html)
